content.full_text:
BOLD AND POLISNER
FREDERICK BOLD, JR.
JEFFREY D. POLISNER
CARL P. A. NELSON
CRAIG L. JUDSON

ATTORNEYS AT LAW
SUITE 325
500 YONACIO VALLEY ROAD
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA 94596-3840

TELEPHONE
(415) 933-7777
FACSIMILE
(415) 933-7804

September 20, 1991

John Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street - Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Re: Contra Costa Water District Request for Information
Assistance - Your File No. I-91-286

Dear Mr. Wallace:

You may recall various telephone conversations that you and I have had in the past concerning the mass-mailing provisions of Proposition 73 and litigation involving that Proposition and Proposition 68. This letter, however, concerns a different subject.

Contra Costa Water District ("CCWD") referred your September 9, 1991 letter to William Woska to my office for review and comment. My office is general counsel for CCWD. We wrote to seek clarification as to Regulation 18700 (a) (2) (2 Code Cal. Regs. §18700) and its application.

A preliminary questions has to do with the use of the term "natural person" in the definition of "consultant" set forth in Regulation 18700 (a) (2). Footnote 2 indicates that "a consultant...cannot be a corporation". Is the converse true (i.e., can a corporation be a consultant)? If a corporation or a partnership could be a consultant, how would the existence of a disclosable economic interest be determined (i.e., by firm or by the natural person actually performing the "consulting" work)?

Second, your letter indicated on page 4 that the definition was intended to avoid the delegation of decision-making authority to private parties such as consultants. On the following page you conclude that "it does not appear that either form makes final decisions for the district." This suggests to us that a disclosure of economic interests would not serve to further the purpose behind subjecting consultants to disclosure procedures.

Third, on page five you conclude "that employees of both firms perform their services in coordination with the district to a greater extent than would be considered mere contract monitoring." We have difficulty understanding the reasons that lead to this conclusion.

BOLD AND POLISNER

Contra Costa Water District
Page Two
September 20, 1991


     With respect to Hasseltine-Best, would the result be
different if the words "under the control" were deleted?  It
appears that the difference between the two firms vis-a-vis CCWD
is the length of the assignments.  If Hasseltine-Best were given
one assignment of reporting information to another party
concerning a CCWD water project with consultation sufficient to
enable the firm to explain in the project accurately and concisely,
and made such a report, and then reported back to CCWD how the
information was received, in your view would such work involve
more control than is represented by "normal contract monitoring"?
If Hasseltine-Best were to be given several such assignments in
succession, would this change the result?

     With respect to McGill-Martin-Self, could you elaborate upon
the basis for your conclusion that the coordination of activities
were more than mere contract monitoring?

     Finally, there appears to be an inconsistency between
independence of the sort that would exempt a "consultant" from
disclosure requirements under Regulation 18700 (a) (2) (A), and
the independence that would make a "consultant" a participant in
the making of governmental decisions under Regulation 18700 (c)
(2).  Could you explain the distinction between these two
situations?

     One final observation: it appears that there are certain
discrepancies between the accounts of the consultants and CCWD
concerning the actual scope of the services provided.  It may be
necessary to clarify this matter and, as suggested in footnote 5
of your letter, contact the Commission for further advice before
the ultimate determination is made.

                              Yours very truly,

                              [signature]

                              Carl P.A. Nelson

CN:jhj

cc:   W.E. Seedmiller
      William Woska
